item.title: How Prediction Markets Turned the World Into a Casino
item.description: As billions pour into platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket, the line between betting and trading is rapidly disappearing.

item.title: Rhee to Deliver Final Rate Decision at a More Transparent BOK
item.description: Rhee Chang Yong will deliver his last rate decision as head of South Korea’s central bank on Friday, capping a tenure defined as much by advances in communication as by policy changes.

item.title: ConocoPhillips Sends Team to Venezuela to Evaluate Oil Prospects
item.description: ConocoPhillips has dispatched a team to Venezuela to evaluate the prospects for a return to drilling in the oil-rich Caribbean nation almost two decades after billions of dollars in assets were seized by the socialist regime.

item.title: Ukrainian Railways Restructuring Proposal Rejected by Investors
item.description: Initial debt-restructuring negotiations between Ukrainian Railways and its bondholders have ended without a deal after investors rejected an opening proposal from the state-owned rail operator, according to statements from both parties.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Ousts Leonardo CEO, Renews Heads of Eni, Enel
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni ousted the chief executive officer of Leonardo SpA, the state-backed aerospace and defense contractor, while retaining CEOs at energy firms Eni SpA and Enel SpA.

item.title: Barclays Sounds Out Investors for a Shutterfly Refinancing Deal
item.description: Barclays PLC is sounding out potential investors to help Shutterfly push out its looming debt pile, after talks with private credit lenders lost momentum.

item.title: OpenAI Tells Investors It Has Computing Advantage Over Anthropic
item.description: OpenAI told investors this week that its early push to dramatically increase computing resources gives it a key advantage over Anthropic PBC at a moment when its longtime rival is gaining ground and mulling a potential public offering.

item.title: Bond Traders Hedge Against More Losses as Inflation Data Looms
item.description: Investors in the $31 trillion Treasury market are going into a closely-watched report on consumer prices hedging against more losses in government bonds, as a fragile truce between the US and Iran takes hold.

item.title: BDC Bust Creates Bond Opportunity for MFS Investment Management
item.description: The retail exodus from business development companies has dragged the vehicles’ debt to levels that are starting to look attractive, according to MFS Investment Management’s Alex Mackey.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Model Heralds New Era for AI Releases
item.description: The startup is rethinking how to safely roll out more powerful technology.

item.title: Texas Landowner’s Stock Sinks as CEO of Largest Shareholder Dies
item.description: Shares of Texas Pacific Land Corp. tumbled Thursday after the chief executive officer of its largest shareholder died, rattling investors in the Texas landowner that has drawn attention as technology companies seek sites for data centers.

item.title: Apollo’s Rowan Resolves JPMorgan Suit Over Charlie Javice Fraud
item.description: Apollo Global Management Chief Executive Officer Marc Rowan resolved a lawsuit JPMorgan Chase & Co. brought against him and other early investors in Charlie Javice’s student-finance startup, Frank.

item.title: Capstone Is Said to Tap Scotiabank to Divest Mexican Copper Mine
item.description: Capstone Copper Corp. is looking to sell a copper mine in Mexico as it focuses growth efforts in Chile, adding to a recent spate of deal-making for the red metal, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Cargill’s Head of World Trading Departs Firm After Three Decades
item.description: Alex Sanfeliu, head of world trading at Cargill Inc., is leaving the top agricultural commodities firm after three decades, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chile Pension Regulator Sets Limit on Swaps That Boosted Returns
item.description: Chile’s pension regulator set new risk-based rules limiting the use of derivatives by pension funds known as AFPs, tightening their scrutiny after foreign interest rate positions helped buttress returns but led to concerns about potential financial strain.

item.title: Iran’s Sanctions-Busting Crypto Ambitions Grow on Toll Payments
item.description: As a fragile US-Iran ceasefire takes shape, Tehran has signaled that payments in digital currency should form part of any toll system for vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz, the chokepoint through which around a fifth of the world’s oil normally flows. The logic is clear: tokens cannot be easily confiscated under sanctions.

item.title: Saudi Output Capacity Cut 600,000 Barrels a Day by War
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s oil production capacity has been cut by nearly 600,000 barrels a day due to a spate of attacks on energy facilities, pipelines and refineries that cost the life of one citizen, the state news agency said.

item.title: UBS Won’t Release Nazi Accounts Settlement Files Sought by Investigator After Court Setback
item.description: UBS Group AG says it won’t hand over a stash of privileged documents for a probe into Credit Suisse’s handling of Nazi-linked accounts after failing to win assurances that doing so wouldn’t expose it to new financial claims.

item.title: Why US-Iran Ceasefire Hasn’t Led to Hormuz Reopening
item.description: Maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz — a vital route for exports of oil, natural gas and other commodities from the Persian Gulf — remains severely constrained by Iran, even after a US-Iran ceasefire took effect on April 8.

item.title: Glencore and Mercuria Agree to Buy More LNG From Commonwealth
item.description: Glencore Plc. and Mercuria Energy Trading SA agreed to increase liquefied natural gas purchases under 20-year deals with an export terminal Kimmeridge Energy Management Co. is developing in Louisiana, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Blackstone Unit Lends $226 Million for US Outdoor Storage Lots
item.description: A unit of Blackstone Inc. provided a $226 million loan for a group of industrial outdoor storage facilities concentrated in the US Sunbelt.

item.title: Venezuelan Cops Block March as Pay Pledge Fails to Calm Workers
item.description: Venezuelan security forces blocked an anti-government march from reaching the presidential palace on Thursday, after a vague pledge to raise wages failed to calm workers angered by soaring prices.

item.title: Carney’s Rival Says He Must Help Homeowners in Indigenous Case
item.description: The political debate over a court ruling recognizing Indigenous land rights in the Vancouver area escalated on Thursday after Conservative Leader Pierre Poilievre urged the prime minister to intervene.

item.title: NLB Joins Raiffeisen in Bidding Battle for Balkans Lender Addiko
item.description: Nova Ljubljanska banka d.d. said it plans to announce a purchase offer for shares in Balkans consumer lending specialist Addiko Bank AG, countering a similar proposal announced less than a day ago by Raiffeisen Bank International AG.

item.title: Nigeria’s Dollar Reserves Dwindle as Central Bank Defends Naira
item.description: Nigeria‘s naira has stayed largely stable through the market volatility sparked by the Iran war, but that came at a cost to the nation’s foreign-exchange reserves.

item.title: Mercuria Trader Who Built Huge LME Aluminum Bets Has Left
item.description: Sonny McNess, the veteran trader who helped build such massive aluminum positions at Mercuria Energy Group that the London Metal Exchange was forced to adjust its rules, has left the trading house.

item.title: Banxico’s Dissenters Warn About Price Pressures Due to Iran War
item.description: Mexico’s central bank’s decision to cut interest rates last week faced strong opposition from two of its five board members, who raised concerns about lingering inflationary pressures from the war in Iran.

item.title: Brazil Weighs Raising Ethanol Blend in Boost to Consumers, Mills
item.description: The world’s second-largest ethanol producer Brazil is considering raising the amount of ethanol in gasoline, in a move that would aid consumers facing higher fuel prices while also boosting demand for sugar-cane mills struggling with a biofuel supply glut.

item.title: FBI Breach Exposes Secret Investigative Records to Intruders
item.description: Prior cyber-espionage campaigns resulted in the theft of sealed records from US courts.

item.title: Ex-First Brands Officer Says He Was Kept in the Dark About Fraud
item.description: First Brands Group’s former chief strategy officer asked a judge to dismiss a civil lawsuit that blames him in part for the auto-parts supplier’s collapse, arguing that founder Patrick James and his brother, Edward, concealed the alleged fraud that brought down the company.
